note: The growing, canonical copy of the course anthology. Add a specimen = append one row and push. Wings: core (the founding collection), acquisition (added during the course), cohort (student work, first names only, accessioned by class vote).

specimens.title: Neural Thickets
specimens.readerAction: Move through a research website with visual metaphors, algorithm explanation, benchmark controls, and links out to arXiv, Colab, and GitHub.
specimens.underneath: A current AI paper presented as an experience: finding, intuition, algorithm, demo, implementation.
specimens.borrow: Click to reveal the landscape — make the thesis visible before the formal method arrives.

specimens.title: Against LLM Reductionism
specimens.readerAction: Read a long public argument with embedded references, examples, images, and linked evidence.
specimens.underneath: A research essay carrying scholarly argument outside a PDF.
specimens.borrow: Claim ladder — each section answers one possible objection.

specimens.title: Don't Train the Model, Evolve the Harness
specimens.readerAction: Encounter a paper-like argument inside a Hugging Face Space — the app surface and the exposition live together.
specimens.underneath: The paper lives inside the tool it describes.
specimens.borrow: App-first introduction — let the reader use the tool, then explain what happened.

specimens.title: How to Use t-SNE Effectively
specimens.readerAction: Watch and compare t-SNE behavior across parameters and examples.
specimens.underneath: A peer-reviewed interactive article; the reader learns why static plots mislead by seeing the algorithm behave.
specimens.borrow: Parameter gallery — the same case under different settings.

specimens.title: Growing Neural Cellular Automata
specimens.readerAction: Damage a pattern, change the model type, and watch the system regenerate.
specimens.underneath: The interaction is the evidence: the reader directly experiences the phenomenon the paper studies.
specimens.borrow: Break it and watch recovery — let the reader perturb the system.

specimens.title: Exploring Neural Networks with Activation Atlases
specimens.readerAction: Inspect a visual atlas of network activations, moving between global overview and local examples.
specimens.underneath: A huge invisible model space turned into a browsable map.
specimens.borrow: Atlas view — arrange many examples spatially, let the reader inspect clusters.

specimens.title: Exploring Bayesian Optimization
specimens.readerAction: Follow stepwise visual explanations of how a model chooses the next point to test.
specimens.underneath: An algorithm made to feel like a decision process, not just math.
specimens.borrow: Algorithm walkthrough — step, explain, visualize, repeat.

specimens.title: Communicating with Interactive Articles
specimens.readerAction: Read a research article about interactive articles while encountering the design patterns it describes.
specimens.underneath: The course's meta-reading: language for why interaction improves learning.
specimens.borrow: Design pattern catalog — name the pattern, show the pattern, say when to use it.

specimens.title: Quantum Computing for the Very Curious
specimens.readerAction: Read an essay with embedded questions and spaced repetition.
specimens.underneath: A paper that tries to change what the reader remembers over time.
specimens.borrow: Memory checkpoint — ask the reader to answer before continuing.

specimens.title: Seeing Theory
specimens.readerAction: Explore probability and statistics through interactive visual chapters.
specimens.underneath: A whole subject re-presented as manipulable visual intuition.
specimens.borrow: Visual concept lab — turn a formula into a small playground.

specimens.title: Boba: Visualizing Multiverse Analyses
specimens.readerAction: Study a system for authoring and reviewing many plausible analysis paths.
specimens.underneath: Research method made inspectable through an interface.
specimens.borrow: Many paths view — show how different choices lead to different results.

specimens.title: Argo Scholar
specimens.readerAction: Explore scholarly literature through an interactive graph connected to live paper data.
specimens.underneath: A paper about an app for exploring papers.
specimens.borrow: Expandable research map — start with one node, grow the field.

specimens.title: HELM: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
specimens.readerAction: Move from a benchmark paper into a living results dashboard.
specimens.underneath: The paper-app as an ongoing public evidence surface.
specimens.borrow: Living benchmark table — give readers filters and comparisons across evidence.

specimens.title: PaperVoyager
specimens.readerAction: Encounter the idea that papers can be converted into executable interactive web systems.
specimens.underneath: The future this course prepares students for: papers becoming experiences, simulations, tools.
specimens.borrow: Mechanism reconstruction — turn a paper's moving parts into controls.

specimens.title: Parable of the Polygons
specimens.readerAction: Drag unhappy shapes into new homes and watch a whole society segregate — then try to undo it.
specimens.underneath: A playable version of Schelling's segregation model; the paper's claim is a game you lose.
specimens.borrow: Argument game — the reader's own play produces the evidence for the thesis.

specimens.title: The Evolution of Trust
specimens.readerAction: Play iterated game-theory tournaments against different strategies and watch cooperation evolve or collapse.
specimens.underneath: Axelrod's tournaments rebuilt as a 30-minute playable essay.
specimens.borrow: Tournament reveal — let the reader bet on a strategy before the simulation runs.

specimens.title: Mechanical Watch
specimens.readerAction: Drag, spin, and disassemble every figure in a long-form explanation of how a watch works.
specimens.underneath: Every diagram is a live 3D simulation; nothing is a static image.
specimens.borrow: Draggable figure — any diagram you were going to paste, make it turnable instead.

specimens.title: The Deep Sea
specimens.readerAction: Scroll downward through the ocean, meter by meter, until the bottom.
specimens.underneath: An argument about scale where the scrollbar itself is the evidence.
specimens.borrow: Scroll essay — let distance, time, or quantity map onto the reader's own scrolling.
